mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 5% indicates tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns falls below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals robust demand conditions that elevate corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling IC < 0.05, or returns are negative in 50%+ of triggering periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% prompts central bank rate hikes that increase equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO > 1.5% MoM, or the IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in consumer finance credit (RSAFS) signals reduced household spending power that weighs on corporate revenues, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS < 330,000, or the correlation between RSAFS and returns is less than -0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low interest rates creates a dual tailwind for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling IC < 0.05, or returns are negative in 50%+ of triggering periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 2% signals weakening demand conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where GDPC1 < 2% YoY, or the correlation between GDPC1 and returns is greater than -0.1.
